fields.icd_conflict_detail: The referral form (Document 1) lists ICD-10 code M54.4 (Lumbago with sciatica), while both the clinical notes (Document 2) and the prescription (Document 3) consistently list M51.16 (Intervertebral disc degeneration, lumbar region). Per resolution protocol, clinical notes and prescription take authority over the referral form; M51.16 was selected as the correct code.

icd_conflicts.label: Primary Diagnosis
icd_conflicts.reasoning: Clinical notes and prescription both consistently document M51.16 (Intervertebral disc degeneration, lumbar region), which takes authority over the referral form's M54.4 (Lumbago with sciatica); confidence is 85 due to the discrepancy with the referral form, but two authoritative documents align on M51.16.

fields.priority_reason: Chronic lumbar intervertebral disc degeneration is a moderate musculoskeletal condition; the ICD conflict was resolved with sufficient confidence (85) and does not require escalation, and a TENS unit is a moderate pain management aid rather than urgent life-sustaining equipment.
fields.notes: 1) Physician NPI is missing/blank across all three documents — must be obtained before billing submission. 2) ICD-10 code conflict exists between referral form (M54.4) and clinical notes/prescription (M51.16); resolved in favor of M51.16 per protocol. 3) Referral was submitted on 07/16/2026, one week after the injury date of 07/09/2026. 4) Prescription is valid for 90 days from 07/16/2026, expiring on 10/14/2026. 5) Expected duration of need is 6 months. 6) Functional assessment notes current assistive devices are insufficient, suggesting possible additional DME needs may arise. 7) Patient BMI is elevated (5'1", 257 lbs) which may be a relevant clinical consideration for equipment specifications.

kg_validation.source: CMS ICD-10-CM Official Guidelines for Coding and Reporting, FY2026

email_body:
Dear James Archer,

I hope this message finds you well. I am reaching out regarding the workers' compensation DME referral for your insured, **Brenda Ramirez**, under claim number **WC-2026-084492**.

Upon reviewing the referral documentation, we have identified the following item that must be provided before we are able to process and route this order:

- **Physician NPI** – The ordering physician's National Provider Identifier (NPI) is missing and is required to complete the referral.

Please note that there are no current hard blocks holding routing at this time; however, we are unable to finalize processing until the above information is received.

We kindly ask that you provide the missing information **within 24 business hours** of receipt of this email to avoid any delays in equipment fulfillment for Ms. Ramirez. You may reply directly to this email with the necessary details.

If you have any questions or need assistance, please do not hesitate to reach out.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Warm regards,

**Coastal DME Intake Agent**
Coastal DME

icd_conflicts.label: Primary Diagnosis
icd_conflicts.reasoning: Clinical notes and prescription both consistently document M51.16 (Intervertebral disc degeneration, lumbar region), which takes authority over the referral form's M54.4 (Lumbago with sciatica); confidence is 85 due to the discrepancy with the referral form, but two authoritative documents align on M51.16.
